Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers, 1945-2019
Collection Number: 3-12-4427

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers, 1945-2019
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
3-12-4427
Abstract:
Frank Harold Trevor Rhodes (1926-2020) was born in Warwickshire, England. The Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers document the early career of Frank Rhodes in the geologic sciences, paleontology, earth science, fossils, conodonts, and later work as an author dating from the 1940's to the 2010's.
Creator:
Rhodes, Frank H. T.
Quanitities:
71 cubic feet.
Access restricted to the permision of the family of Frank Rhodes.
Biographical / Historical

Frank Harold Trevor Rhodes was born in Warwickshire, England, on Oct. 29, 1926. He earned his bachelor's, doctor of science and doctor of philosophy degrees in geology from the University of Birmingham, England. He taught at the University of Durham and the University of Wales, Swansea, where he also was dean of the faculty of science. He was a life fellow of Clare Hall, Cambridge, a visiting fellow of Trinity College, Oxford, and an honorary fellow of Robinson College, Cambridge.
In the United States, Rhodes taught at the University of Illinois and Ohio State University (as a Fulbright scholar and a National Science Foundation senior visiting research fellow, respectively) before joining the faculty at the University of Michigan in 1968 as a professor of geology. In 1971, he was named dean of Michigan's College of Literature, Science and the Arts and, from 1974-77, he served as Michigan's vice president for academic affairs.
He served as Cornell University President from 1977 to 1995, and maintained a role as a Presidential liaison in his emeritus capacity the rest of his life. He also continued to write and teach in the geological sciences and as a lifelong paleontologist and Darwin scholar.
He served on numerous boards including as trustee of King Abdullah University of Science and Technology.
He passed away February 3, 2020.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Preferred Citation

Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers, #3-12-4427. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials

See Cornell Adult University records, #41-8-3726, for a CD of Rhodes trip to Japan, China, and Vietnam with CAU.
Content Description

The Frank Rhodes personal and geological sciences papers document the early career of Frank Rhodes (1926-2020) in the geologic sciences, paleontology, earth science, fossils, conodonts, and later work as an author dating from the 1940's to the 2010's. In contains publications, drafts and research notes relating to books, speeches, slides, correspondence. Items pertaining to King Abdullah University of Science and Technology. Cornell Adult University teaching materials for courses he led. Items relating to his time at Birmingham, Swansea, Ohio, and Michigan.
SUBJECTS

Names:
Rhodes, Frank H. T.
Darwin, Charles, 1809-1882.
King Abdullah University of Science and Technology
Subjects:
Geology.
Physical/Earth Sciences
Natural Resources Conservation and Protection
Conodonts
Higher Education
CONTAINER LIST
Container
Description
Date
Series I. Pre-Cornell Work
 1942-1978
Scope and Contents
Contains records from Rhodes' studies and career prior to his arrival at Cornell. Includes records of his undergraduate and graduate studies at the University of Birmingham, his teaching/research at the University of Durham, University of Illinois, the University of Wales, Swansea, and Ohio State University. This series also includes records pertaining to his work at the University of Michigan, first as professor of geology and then as Dean of the College of Literature, Science and the Arts.

I.d. Correspondence
 1949-1989
Processing Information
Correspondence in this subseries was consolidated by processing staff. Folders named after individuals by Rhodes were preserved, while loose correspondence was consolidated into chronological folders. In some cases, material extends beyond the scope of this series and into Rhodes' time at Cornell. As this material was stored with early material and in some cases interfiled with earlier correspondence, it was decided to store and arrange all of the correspondence together in this subseries.

 1942-1945
Series II. Cornell Activities
 1970-2014
Scope and Contents
Contains materials documenting Rhodes' work at Cornell outside of his Presidential duties. Includes records of his inauguration and early years at Cornell, drafts and correspondence relating to his course lectures and teaching, research materials, and retirement. Also includes records documenting Cornell Adult University (CAU) trips led by Rhodes.

Series III. Publications and Writing
 1952-2017
Scope and Contents
Includes originals, photocopies, drafts, research, and correspondence relating to Rhodes' published articles and books.
III.a. Articles
 1952-2015
Arranged chronologically by publication date. Co-authors and publisher are included in Scope and Content notes.
Processing Information
Rhodes' papers originally included two distinct series of articles. One series included publications and research/draft material while another contained photocopies and research/draft material. These series were consolidated and duplicates were weeded.
Box 1 Folder 1
A Classification of Pennsylvanian Conodont Assemblages

 1955

Series IV. Speeches
 1969-2017
Scope and Contents
Primarily contains speeches given by Rhodes. Also includes speeches given by others, research and speech prep, and correspondence relating to speaking engagements.
Arranged chronologically by date speech was given by Rhodes.
IV.a. Speeches
 1969-2017

Box 31
Quotes
 Undated
Scope and Contents
Contains clippings of quotations removed from card files.
Rhodes' alphabetical subject listing was preserved.
Box 32
Quotes
 Undated
Scope and Contents
Contains clippings of quotations removed from card files and binders. Includes quotes described by Rhodes as serious, humor, and travel quotes.
Rhodes' alphabetical subject listing was preserved.
Series V. Office Files
 1995-2019
Scope and Contents
Contains office files related to Rhodes' professional activities (primarily) following his retirement from Cornell. Includes research on higher education and various universities, donor correspondence, planning for the Cornell University Medical Center (CUMC), events (including class reunions), professorships and scholarships, and records of Rhodes' work in the American Philosophical Society (APS).
Arrangement reflects Rhodes' original order of series and subseries.

V.l. The Atlantic Philanthropies
1997-2009
Scope and Contents
Materials kept by Rhodes during his time on the Atlantic Philanthropies Board of Trustees, which he served from 1995 until 2008 and was Chair from 2000 until 2008. Most records in this sub-series are from a Board trip to Cape Town South Africa in 2005.

Box 75
Honorary doctorates and membership certificates
 1976-2002
Scope and Contents
Honorary doctorates from a number of colleges and universities including Clemson University, Ohio State, and the University of Sydney. Box also includes other honors such as a 1979 certificate proclaiming Rhodes an honrary citizen of the city of Dallas.
